items.title: [Engadget] US government allows Anthropic to redeploy its Mythos and Fable AI models
items.content_html: Anthropic will start its users' access to Mythos and Fable tomorrow, July 1.

items.title: [Business Insider] Anthropic to restore access to Fable 5 after negotiations with White House
items.content_html: Anthropic said it had to "abruptly disable" access to Fable 5, its most powerful publicly available AI model, after a Trump administration order.

items.title: 🔓 [NYT Business] Neon Buys ‘Artificial,’ a Film About OpenAI, After Amazon Dropped It
items.content_html: Neon purchased “Artificial,” which focuses on OpenAI’s chief, Sam Altman, after Amazon walked away from it following an investment in the start-up.

items.title: [STAT News] STAT+: AI company Anthropic announces it will begin developing drugs of its own
items.content_html: AI giant Anthropic has already become a dominant player in technology and a household name for everyday users of artificial intelligence. Can it make drugs too?

items.title: [STAT News] STAT+: Anthropic releases Claude Science, a product aimed at researchers, the pharma industry
items.content_html: Anthropic released Claude Science, an application that optimizes its large language model for scientists and, especially, those doing research at pharma companies.

items.title: [Tom's Hardware] Chinese Z.ai's latest model tops AI ranking charts amid Anthropic Fable 5 ban — blacklisted China firm's popular open-weight GLM-5.2 AI model powered by Huawei silicon
items.content_html: Within a week of Fable's ban, GLM-5.2 had climbed to the top of the openly available leaderboards.

items.title: [TechCrunch] Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
items.content_html: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

items.title: [Android Authority] Gemini’s smartest image feature is no longer locked behind a paywall
items.content_html: Gemini can now pull your photos and search history to generate custom images for free.

items.title: 🔓 [WIRED] Meta Contractors Posed as Teens to Prompt Rival Chatbots About Suicide, Sex, and Drugs
items.content_html: Hundreds of contractors working on a project for Meta pretended to be kids in order to see how other chatbots like Gemini and ChatGPT would respond to high-risk subjects, WIRED found.

items.title: [Android Authority] Gemini ‘Personal snapshot’ will make it a breeze to review what it knows about you
items.content_html: Gemini will show exactly what it knows about you and let you make corrections.

items.title: [Android Authority] The Gemini overlay could soon make your recent chats much easier to reach
items.content_html: Our latest APK teardown spotted a new Agent Dashboard chip on the overlay that leads to recent conversations.

items.title: [Engadget] Google expands personalized intelligence to Gemini app image creation
items.content_html: Google expands personalized intelligence to Gemini app image creation

items.title: [Neowin] Amazon may use OpenAI and Nova models after Anthropic reportedly raises costs
items.content_html: Amazon is reportedly considering greater use of OpenAI models and its own Nova family after Anthropic raised the cost of using its models in Amazon products. Read more...

items.title: [Neowin] Google Meet brings Gemini note-taking to AI Pro and Ultra subscribers
items.content_html: Google is expanding its Gemini-powered note-taking feature called "Take notes for me" in Meet to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers. Read more...

items.title: [Toms Guide] Google couldn't give Meta enough AI power — here's why running AI locally suddenly makes even more sense
items.content_html: Google reportedly couldn't meet Meta's demand for Gemini AI compute. It's another reminder that local AI may become one of the biggest trends in personal computing.

items.title: [CNET] Bots Now Outnumber Humans Online. Here's Why It Matters.
items.content_html: Cloudflare says AI agents now generate most web traffic, marking a historic shift that could reshape how websites, businesses, and people interact online.

items.title: 🔓 [NYT Business] In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
items.content_html: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

items.title: [TechRadar] 'The goal is not to replace humans': new Meta AI research chief Dawn Song says the next frontier is AI agents that are "economically valuable"
items.content_html: Meta's AI Research VP believes AI should augment human value and that many benchmarks are irrelevant as companies struggle to prove ROI.

items.title: [Engadget] Don't like Gemini? Here's how to roll back to Google Assistant on your Android phone
items.content_html: Want to go back to your ex... AI assistant? We don't blame you. Take these steps to get Google Assistant back after your fling with Gemini.

items.title: [TechRadar] How AI observability helps organizations move from experimentation to production
items.content_html: AI observability prevents invisible drift and reduces AI overheads to deliver multiple model, agent driven systems.

items.title: [Business Insider] OpenAI sets up 'warroom' to investigate users burning through Codex credits faster than usual
items.content_html: OpenAI investigates Codex's usage limit depletion that's impacting some users. The company has reset user caps to address the problem.

items.title: [Engadget] Google reportedly capped Meta's use of Gemini AI for coding and chatbots
items.content_html: Google was forced to cap Meta's use of Gemini AI due to a lack of capacity.

items.title: [Neowin] Microsoft Edge gets tons of security features, including AI model that can see your screen
items.content_html: Edge for Business is getting smarter enterprise protections, but one new AI-powered security feature may have some IT admins pondering over its enforcement too. Read more...

items.title: [Kagi Small Web] Same rows, different SUM
items.content_html: Everyone knows not to store money as a double precision . One can hope. The rule is so well drilled that it has stopped being interesting, and it is also not where the trouble usually starts. The float is already in the schema before anyone weighs in on it: a measurement column someone later sums for a report, telemetry that drifts into a finance dashboard, a third-party feed ingested as double precision because that is how it arrived. Apple Paul Meade, an Apple VP who heads the Vision Products Group, is reportedly leaving the company next week for OpenAI. According to Bloomberg, the top executive in charge of the Vision Pro headset and Apple's smart glasses projects will be starting up the AI company's hardware unit. items.content_html: Not long after the generative AI boom took the business world and Wall Street by storm, a chorus of complaints started being leveled against companies like OpenAI, Microsoft, Anthropic, and Google, whose models were trained using a sizable chunk of all the content that’s ever been published on the internet—including, as many subsequent lawsuits would allege, massive quantities of copyrighted materials.
